Never before have we seen demand like this.
Not only did we recover, but we also grew stronger.
Only by taking bold risks did we build this momentum.
Andrea, you're closing our tourism summit. In one line -- what's the vision you want to leave the room with?
That we stopped following the market and started leading it. Never before have we had a chance like the one in front of us.
Strong opening. But the last two years were brutal for the industry. Won't that ring hollow?
No -- I'll name it. Not only did we survive the downturn, but we also came out with better partners and a bolder plan. The struggle is the point.
And how do you get them to actually follow you out of the room?
By making the aspiration concrete. I envision us as the region's most trusted operator within three years -- and I'll tell them exactly how we get there.
That's a legacy worth chasing. How do you want them to feel as they walk out?
Like it's already happening. Only by moving together did we get this far -- and rarely do you get a moment this good to build on. Let's not waste it.
Never before have we seen this.
Not only did we recover.
Only by moving together did we get here.
Rarely do you get a moment this good.
Let me tell you where we're going.
Never before have we had a chance like this.
Not only did we survive, but we also grew.
I envision us as the region's most trusted operator.
That is the legacy I want us to build.

I want to close by telling you where we're going. But first, let me tell you where we were. Two years ago, this industry was on its knees. Bookings vanished, partners pulled out, and there were mornings I wasn't sure we'd make it. I won't pretend that was easy, because resilience doesn't mean anything if the struggle wasn't real. And then came the turning point. We made one bold decision to stop competing on price and start leading on trust. Never before have we grown the way we've grown since. Not only did we recover, but we also came out with better partners and a clearer purpose than we've ever had. So here is the vision. Within three years, I want us to be the most trusted operator in the region, the name people reach for first. That is the legacy I want us to build together. And here is the thing about a moment like this. Rarely do you get a chance this good. So let's not applaud it. Let's go and make it inevitable.

Let's talk about inversion for emphasis, the grammar of great speeches. Normally, English word order is subject first, then verb. We have never seen demand like this. That's fine, but it's flat. Now watch what happens when you take the negative phrase and move it to the front. Never before have we seen demand like this. Did you feel that? When a negative or limiting phrase goes first, the word order flips. The auxiliary jumps in front of the subject, exactly like a question. Have we, not we have. Did we, not we did. It works with never, rarely, seldom, little, and with not only, followed by but also. It works with only, as in only by working together did we get here. One warning. This is a spice, not a sauce. Use it once or twice, at the peak moments where you want the room to lean in. Invert every sentence and it stops sounding powerful and starts sounding strange. Place it well, and you can make a simple claim feel like history.
